summary:
The primary source for progenitor hematopoietic stem cell origin shifts from the yolk sac to the fetal liver before finally settling into the bone marrow as a fetus develops toward full-term pregnancy. Agent1 reasoned based on the provided information regarding when these cells develop within different organs throughout prenatal stages leading up to birth. Key point is focusing primarily on where such cells are most prominent by late-stage fetal life or shortly after delivery which aligns with their function post-birth. 

-Agent1
-
